title: JOHN AT THE ROBERTS
subtitle: CAMEO · VI·Lovers · 6:42 PM · The night before the Polaroid
epigraph_upright: John drives over from the diner with a half-eaten slice of pie wrapped in foil and a sentence he's been turning around all afternoon. The Polaroid is, unbeknownst to anyone in this house, in a wagon at the Theriot's two streets over.
epigraph_reversed: John drives over with the pie. He sits in the car at the curb for eleven minutes. He drives home. The half-eaten slice goes back in the diner's fridge by 7:30.
scene_description: Medium cameo. The Fool at the Lovers' threshold the night before the canonical boy-with-the-Polaroid. John doesn't know what's coming. The room doesn't either. Something he says or doesn't say will, by tomorrow, matter.
direction_hint: Sit on the back porch with Philip. Leave the napkin on the kitchen counter. Wait for the Polaroid to reveal at doubt 5. Leap.
opening_log_lines: 6:42 PM. John at the Roberts' front walk with the foil-wrapped pie.
Mackenzie at the kitchen window. Philip in the back yard.
The sentence you have been turning all afternoon is, this evening, still not the right one.

scenario_action_cards.title: SIT ON THE BACK PORCH WITH PHILIP
scenario_action_cards.flavor: You bring two beers. Philip in the lawn chair. You sit. You do not, this evening, mention the sentence you have been turning. You let Philip do the talking.

scenario_action_cards.title: LEAVE THE NAPKIN ON THE COUNTER
scenario_action_cards.flavor: You write the half-sentence on a napkin. You leave it on the kitchen counter between the salt and the basil pot. Mackenzie will, by morning, find it.

scenario_visitors.name: The Polaroid (not yet)
scenario_visitors.lore_text: A forthcoming visitor. Not on the board at start. Reveals at doubt 5 — at which point John registers, without being able to name, the Theriot's wagon two streets over.

steps.sense: You feel, at the front door, that something is about to be on this porch tomorrow.
steps.wait: You stand on the front walk for a full minute. The street is the street.
steps.name_it: You almost name it. You don't. You go back inside.
steps.leave_room_for_it: You leave the house the way you came. You do not, this evening, tell Mackenzie. You leave room for tomorrow's news to arrive on its own.

scenario_visitors.name: Philip (in the lawn chair)
scenario_visitors.lore_text: Philip Roberts. Two beers in. The lawn chair. The basil from Mackenzie's garden is, this June, doing well.

steps.greet: Philip raises the beer. He nods to the empty chair. You sit.
steps.listen: He tells you the small story of Mackenzie's brother that he tells exactly four times a year, always between the second and third beer.
steps.deliver: You give him the small piece of news you came with — the editor at the paper liked the column. He says: I knew he would.
steps.sit_with: You sit through twenty minutes of him not saying anything. The cicadas are at dusk-frequency. The third beer does not, this evening, get opened.

stagnation_thresholds.label: the front walk. the foil-wrapped pie.
the sentence is still not the right sentence.
philip has, by year three, learned to wait you out.
you drove back to the diner without going in.

doubt_thresholds.label: john at the roberts' front door.
the Theriot's wagon, two streets over, is the Theriot's wagon.
you almost named what is coming.
the polaroid arrives in the morning regardless.

notes: Medium cameo · The night before. Novel mechanic: DEFERRED-REVEAL VISITOR. the_polaroid_about_to_arrive is not on the board at start; doubt level 5 triggers its arrival next turn (engine TODO: honor effect.kind 'deferred_visitor_arrives' on doubt thresholds).
ambient_pool: the basil pot on the kitchen counter is healthy this june.
philip's third beer is, by year three, almost never opened.
